dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 15, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Diamondbacks Outlast Orioles in Thrilling Affair**

In a back-and-forth contest, the Arizona Diamondbacks emerged victorious, topping the Baltimore Orioles 8-5. Adrian Del Castillo led the charge for the D-backs, notching a pair of hits, including a home run, and driving in five runs. Kevin Ginkel proved to be a key contributor out of the bullpen, striking out four in two scoreless innings.

**Red Sox Roll Past Twins in Dominant Performance**

The Boston Red Sox continued their winning ways, defeating the Minnesota Twins 9-5 behind a strong outing from Connelly Early. Early tossed six scoreless frames, striking out five and allowing just two hits. Trevor Story also had a big day, collecting two hits, including a home run, and driving in five runs.

**Cubs Cruise Past Phillies in Blowout Fashion**

The Chicago Cubs made quick work of the Philadelphia Phillies, winning 11-2 behind a strong team effort. Nico Hoerner led the charge, going 3-for-5 with a home run and five RBIs. Shota Imanaga was nearly unhittable on the mound, striking out 11 in six scoreless innings.

**Astros Shut Down Rockies in Low-Scoring Affair**

The Houston Astros took down the Colorado Rockies 3-1 behind a strong outing from Spencer Arrighetti. Arrighetti tossed six scoreless frames, striking out 10 and allowing just two hits. Yordan Alvarez also had a big day, collecting two hits, including a home run, and driving in one run.

**Padres Edge Mariners in Thrilling Finish**

The San Diego Padres edged out the Seattle Mariners 7-6 in a back-and-forth contest. Luke Raley led the charge for the Padres, going 4-for-5 with a home run and two RBIs. Emerson Hancock pitched well for the Mariners, striking out six in six innings.

**Athletics Outlast Rangers in Walk-Off Fashion**

The Oakland Athletics took down the Texas Rangers 6-5 in a thrilling walk-off finish. Jake Burger led the charge for the Athletics, homering in the bottom of the ninth to give his team the win. Kumar Rocker pitched well for the Rangers, striking out six in 4.2 innings.
